---
title: "Implementation map"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Implementation map}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This map answers one practical question: what model surface can an applied user
fit today, and which neighbouring syntax is still planned? It is a status
ledger rather than a tutorial. Start here when a model combines random effects,
structured dependence, response-specific scale, zero inflation, hurdle
probability, or `corpairs()`.

The words below are used literally. "Fitted" means likelihood code, parser
support, extractors or diagnostics, tests, and user-facing documentation are in
place. "Planned" means the package may reserve syntax or describe the design,
but an analysis should not interpret that surface yet.

| Status word | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Stable | Routine fitted path with tests, diagnostics or interval status, and a reader-facing example or guide. |
| First slice | Fitted and tested inside a deliberately narrow boundary. Stay inside the named family, formula, q, and data-shape limits. |
| Fixed-effect only | Formula coefficients are fitted for this distributional parameter, but random effects or structured dependence for that parameter are not fitted. |
| Planned or reserved | Syntax, roadmap wording, or a parser guard may exist, but `drmTMB()` should reject it or treat it as design-only until likelihood, tests, docs, and after-task evidence land. |
| Unsupported or blocked | Do not use as analysis syntax; choose the nearest fitted route or check the roadmap. |

Location means the expected response (`mu`, `mu1`, `mu2`). Scale means residual
standard deviation, dispersion, or an equivalent response-family scale
(`sigma`, `sigma1`, `sigma2`). Shape means a family-specific tail, cutpoint, or
overdispersion control such as Student-t `nu`. Coscale means the residual
correlation layer, currently `rho12` in bivariate Gaussian models.

## Family and component map

Use this table before combining a family with a random-effect or dependence
surface.

| Model family or component | Fitted formulas today | Random effects today | Dependence or correlation today | Main planned boundary |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Gaussian, one response | `mu` and `sigma` | ordinary `mu` intercepts, independent slopes, one-slope correlated blocks, q > 2 numeric `mu` blocks, `sigma` intercepts, independent `sigma` slopes, and `sd(group) ~ x` for unlabelled `mu` intercept SDs | ordinary group-level `corpairs()` rows for fitted covariance blocks | correlated `sigma` slopes, coefficient-specific `sd(group, coef = "x")`, and broad p8 location-scale slope endpoints |
| Student-t | fixed-effect `mu`, `sigma`, and `nu` | none | none | `nu` random effects, skewness, and structured Student-t effects |
| Lognormal, Gamma, beta, beta-binomial | fixed-effect `mu` and `sigma` or the family-specific scale | none | none | scale random effects, bounded-response known covariance, zero-one inflation, and structured dependence |
| Poisson and NB2 | fixed-effect `mu`; NB2 also has fixed-effect `sigma` | ordinary non-zero-inflated `mu` random intercepts and independent numeric slopes | no labelled covariance, no structured dependence | correlated count slopes, zero-inflated or hurdle random effects, count `sigma` random effects, and structured count effects |
| Zero-inflated Poisson and zero-inflated NB2 | fixed-effect `mu`; `zi ~ ...`; NB2 also has fixed-effect `sigma` | none | none | random effects in `zi`, structured zero inflation, and `corpairs()` for count latent effects |
| Truncated NB2 and hurdle NB2 | fixed-effect `mu`; fixed-effect `sigma`; hurdle NB2 also has `hu ~ ...` | none | none | random effects in `hu`, random effects in truncated or hurdle count `mu`, and structured hurdle effects |
| Cumulative-logit ordinal | fixed-effect ordinal location with estimated cutpoints | none | none | ordinal random effects, scale or discrimination formulas, structured ordinal effects, and bivariate ordinal models |
| Gaussian meta-analysis | `meta_V(V = V)` or compatibility alias `meta_known_V(V = V)` | ordinary Gaussian random effects may be combined with estimated residual terms where supported | known sampling covariance is observation-level input, not a latent relatedness effect | non-Gaussian known covariance, proportional-variance `meta_V(w = w)`, sparse/block-sparse known covariance, and dense known `V` with non-unit weights |
| Bivariate Gaussian | `mu1`, `mu2`, `sigma1`, `sigma2`, and fixed-effect `rho12` | ordinary bivariate random-intercept slices, same-response `mu`/`sigma` intercept covariance, all-four q=4 intercept blocks, and matching slope-only `mu1`/`mu2` blocks | residual `rho12()` and group-level `corpairs()` stay separate | random effects in `rho12`, mixed-response bivariate families, intercept-plus-slope q=4 blocks, and all-four p8/q8 slope endpoints |

`zi` and `hu` are currently fixed-effect probability components. They are
useful for modelling extra zeros or hurdle probability, but they should not be
read as random-effect or structured-dependence layers yet.

## Random-effect and dependence map

Here q is the number of latent endpoints in one covariance block. A q=2 block
has two endpoints, such as `mu1` and `mu2` random intercepts. A q=4 block has
four endpoints, such as `mu1`, `mu2`, `sigma1`, and `sigma2` random intercepts.
The p8/q8 language in the roadmap refers to future all-endpoint location-scale
slope structures, not a fitted user route.

| Layer | Fitted q and slope support | Main extractors or diagnostics | Planned or blocked neighbours |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ordinary Gaussian `mu` | q=1 random intercepts and independent slopes; q=2 intercept-slope correlations; q > 2 numeric `mu` blocks | `sdpars$mu`, `corpars$mu` or `corpars$re_cov`, `ranef()`, `corpairs()`, `summary()$covariance`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | q > 2 correlations are derived-unavailable for direct profile intervals and larger q blocks remain advanced |
| Ordinary Gaussian `sigma` | q=1 residual-scale intercepts and multiple independent numeric slopes on `log(sigma)` | `sdpars$sigma`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()`, `sigma()` | correlated or labelled residual-scale slope covariance |
| Random-effect SD surface | `sd(group) ~ x_group` for unlabelled Gaussian `mu` random intercepts | `predict_parameters(dpar = "sd(group)")`, `marginal_parameters()`, fixed-effect summaries | coefficient-specific `sd(group, coef = "x")`, spatial/animal/relmat direct-SD siblings, and generic `sd*()` unification |
| Ordinary bivariate `mu1`/`mu2` | q=2 matching labelled random intercepts and q=2 matching slope-only random-slope blocks | `corpairs(class = "mean-mean")`, `summary()$covariance`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | intercept-plus-slope q=4 location blocks and predictor-dependent slope `corpair()` regressions |
| Ordinary bivariate `sigma1`/`sigma2` | q=2 matching labelled random intercepts | `corpairs(class = "scale-scale")`, `summary()$covariance`, `profile_targets()` | scale random slopes and scale-slope covariance |
| Same-response `mu`/`sigma` | one or more q=2 matching random-intercept blocks within a response | `corpairs(class = "mean-scale")`, `corpars$mu_sigma`, `summary()$covariance` | slope-level mean-scale covariance |
| All-four ordinary location-scale | constant q=4 random-intercept block across `mu1`, `mu2`, `sigma1`, and `sigma2` | `corpairs()` and `summary()$covariance` report six derived latent correlations | derived q=4 interval profiles and p8/q8 random-slope endpoints |
| Residual coscale | fixed-effect `rho12 ~ ...` | `rho12()`, `confint(..., parm = "rho12", newdata = ...)`, `summary()` | random effects or structured dependence in `rho12` |
| Ordinary `corpair()` regression | q=2 predictor-dependent group-level correlation for an already fitted block | `corpairs(newdata = ...)`, `plot_corpairs()` | q=4 correlation regressions and slope-level `corpair()` regressions |
| Phylogenetic `mu` | q=1 univariate intercept and one numeric slope; q=2 bivariate `mu1`/`mu2`; constant q=4 location-scale intercept block | `sdpars$mu`, `ranef("phylo_mu")`, `corpairs(level = "phylogenetic")`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | multiple phylogenetic slopes, phylogenetic slope correlations, standalone phylogenetic `sigma`, structured `rho12`, and non-Gaussian phylogenetic effects |
| Phylogenetic direct SD | `sd_phylo()`, `sd_phylo1()`, and `sd_phylo2()` direct-SD surfaces where documented | fixed-effect summaries, prediction helpers, profile targets where direct | generic `sd*()` naming across phylo, spatial, animal, and `relmat()` remains a future unification lane |
| Coordinate spatial `mu` | q=1 univariate intercept and one numeric slope; q=2 bivariate `mu1`/`mu2` intercept covariance | `sdpars$mu`, `ranef("spatial_mu")`, `corpairs(level = "spatial")`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | mesh/SPDE, multiple slopes, slope correlations, spatial `sigma`, spatial q=4, direct-SD surfaces, and spatial `corpair()` regression |
| Animal-model `mu` | q=1 univariate intercept and one numeric slope for `pedigree`, `A`, or `Ainv`; q=2 bivariate `mu1`/`mu2`; constant q=4 location-scale intercept block | `sdpars$mu`, `ranef("animal_mu")`, `corpairs(level = "animal")`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | sparse large-pedigree construction, multiple slopes, slope correlations, standalone `sigma`, predictor-dependent `corpair()` regression, direct-SD grammar, and non-Gaussian animal effects |
| `relmat()` known latent relatedness | q=1 univariate intercept and one numeric slope for `K` or `Q`; q=2 bivariate `mu1`/`mu2`; constant q=4 location-scale intercept block | `sdpars$mu`, `ranef("relmat_mu")`, `corpairs(level = "relmat")`, `profile_targets()`, `check_drm()` | multiple slopes, slope correlations, standalone `sigma`, predictor-dependent `corpair()` regression, direct-SD grammar, and non-Gaussian `relmat()` effects |
| Non-Gaussian ordinary `mu` | Poisson and NB2 q=1 random intercepts and independent numeric slopes | `sdpars$mu`, `ranef()`, direct `log_sd_mu` profile targets, `check_drm()` | correlated non-Gaussian slopes, labelled covariance, structured dependence, zero-inflated/hurdle random effects, and cross-parameter covariance |
| Non-Gaussian scale, shape, zero-inflation, and hurdle | fixed-effect formulas only where the family supports the component | fixed-effect coefficient tables and Wald intervals where implemented | random effects in `sigma`, `nu`, `zi`, `hu`, future `zoi`, future `coi`, or latent skewness |

## Slope coverage at a glance

| Random-effect type | At least one random slope fitted? | Current fitted scope | Not yet fitted |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ordinary Gaussian `mu` | Yes | independent slopes, one-slope correlated blocks, q > 2 numeric `mu` blocks | routine high-q teaching and direct q > 2 correlation profiles |
| Ordinary Gaussian `sigma` | Yes | independent residual-scale slopes | correlated residual-scale slopes |
| Ordinary bivariate Gaussian | Yes | matching slope-only `mu1`/`mu2` q=2 block | intercept-plus-slope q=4, same-response slope covariance, and p8/q8 all-endpoint slopes |
| Coordinate spatial | Yes | one univariate Gaussian `mu` slope | multiple slopes, slope correlations, q=4 spatial blocks, and spatial `sigma` |
| Phylogenetic | Yes | one univariate Gaussian `mu` slope | multiple slopes, slope correlations, standalone `sigma`, and non-Gaussian effects |
| Animal-model relatedness | Yes | one univariate Gaussian `mu` slope | sparse large-pedigree slope scaling, multiple slopes, slope correlations, and standalone `sigma` |
| `relmat()` known latent relatedness | Yes | one univariate Gaussian `mu` slope | multiple slopes, slope correlations, standalone `sigma`, and direct-SD grammar |
| Non-Gaussian ordinary count `mu` | Yes | Poisson and NB2 independent numeric slopes | correlated slopes, zero-inflated or hurdle random effects, and structured count slopes |
| Non-Gaussian scale, shape, `zi`, `hu`, ordinal, or bounded-response components | No | fixed effects only where implemented | random effects and structured dependence |

## Roadmap use

The next implementation work should move only one boundary at a time. These are
the most useful next lanes for users:

| Slice | Roadmap lane | Why it helps users | Done when |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 303 | Generic `sd*()` design | Users should not need separate direct-SD names for phylo, spatial, animal, and `relmat()` routes forever. | The grammar, examples, tests, and stale-name migration path are explicit without breaking current `sd_phylo*()` users. |
| 304 | p8/q8 location-scale planning | Full individual-difference location-scale slope models need a design before code. | The endpoint labels, covariance block size, positive-definite parameterization, diagnostics, and profile/derived interval policy are written before fitting. |
| 305 | Spatial and relatedness q=4 parity | Users can ask location-scale structured questions beyond phylogeny and ordinary groups. | q=4 spatial, animal, and `relmat()` claims have likelihood code, tests, `corpairs()`, diagnostics, and docs. |
| 306 | q=4 interval policy | Users need to know when q=4 correlations are estimates only and when intervals are real. | Derived q=4 rows keep explicit unavailable interval status until direct or derived-profile interval evidence exists. |
| 307 | Inflation and hurdle random-effect gate | Users with `zi`, `hu`, future `zoi`, or future `coi` models need clear fixed-effect guidance before extra latent layers are added. | These components remain fixed-effect-only or planned until use cases, recovery tests, diagnostics, prediction semantics, and interval-status rules justify random effects. |
| 308 | Non-Gaussian structured-dependence candidate map | Count, ordinal, inflation, and hurdle users need honest guidance before structured models are exposed. | One family and one dependence layer pass likelihood, recovery, diagnostics, extractor, and interval-status gates before a broader claim appears. |
| 309 | Reader-facing availability map maintenance | The pkgdown site should make fitted-versus-planned status easy to find. | This article, `model-map`, README, ROADMAP, NEWS, and the reference index agree after each substantial feature slice. |
| 310 | User-route examples | Users should leave the map knowing the nearest fitted alternative. | Planned rows point to a fitted route, a design note, or a tutorial boundary rather than leaving users at a dead end. |
